methodsAttempted.query: site:griffith.edu.au Griffith University generative AI assessment academic integrity ChatGPT policy students staff
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search surfaced Griffith Library generative AI guidance, the central academic integrity page, an eResearch artificial intelligence page, a Library blog item, and a research-output PDF.
Direct HTTP fetches to official Griffith pages and robots.txt returned a Cloudflare managed challenge, so Firecrawl was used for official-page extraction without attempting to bypass the challenge.

methodsAttempted.query: site:griffith.edu.au/eresearch-services artificial intelligence Copilot DeepSeek public data research Griffith
methodsAttempted.notes: The official eResearch Services artificial intelligence page provided AI tool and research data classification guidance.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found Griffith's Library generative AI guidance for students, the central academic integrity page, and the eResearch Services artificial intelligence page. Direct HTTP was blocked by Cloudflare challenge pages, so successful snapshots are based on Firecrawl fetches of official Griffith URLs. A Library blog item and a narrow research-output PDF were rejected for staged claims because they were advisory or too narrow for broad university claims.

stopConditions: Stop after official Griffith pages support narrow student, academic-integrity, and research/tool claim candidates.
Do not infer binding assessment permission beyond the Library and academic-integrity wording.
Do not bypass Cloudflare, SharePoint, ServiceNow, or LMS login requirements.

artifacts.claimText: Griffith's student-facing generative AI guidance says students may use generative AI for self-study without citation, but for assessment tasks they must acknowledge its use and include a short declaration if they use it while completing the task.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Griffith's academic integrity page states that uncited content created using generative artificial intelligence software is conduct that always constitutes Academic Misconduct, and that representing AI-generated assessment content as a student's own work is Academic Misconduct.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Griffith says it provides free access to Microsoft Copilot for staff and students and describes it as the preferred generative AI tool because signed-in Griffith-account use operates as a closed system with protected prompts and responses.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Griffith's student generative AI guidance warns that many open or public tools do not guarantee confidentiality and advises users not to enter personal information, information about others, or course materials such as assessment tasks or marking rubrics.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Griffith eResearch guidance says Microsoft Copilot is approved for Unofficial, Official (Public), Official (Internal), and Sensitive data classifications when users log in with a Griffith account, while Protected data is not approved for Microsoft Copilot.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Griffith eResearch guidance says ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok and other AI chat tools are not individually approved, but may be used for research purposes involving Public Data only, and says DeepSeek is banned at Griffith University.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Generative AI can be used for self-study without citation. However, for assessments you must acknowledge its use and ensure the accuracy and quality of your submissions. If you use generative AI at any point in completing your assessment tasks, make sure you include a short declaration with your submission explaining how you used it.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Learning and assessments; Acknowledging generative AI use
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Types of conduct that always constitute Academic Misconduct include: uncited content created using generative artificial intelligence software. Uncited content created by generative artificial intelligence (AI) software involves using AI tools, such as ChatGPT, which can be used to generate content for assessment items. Representing AI-generated content as a student's own work is Academic Misconduct.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Griffith provides free access to Microsoft Copilot for staff and students. It is the preferred tool because it operates as a closed system. When you sign in with your Griffith credentials, your data is protected. This means your prompts and responses are not used to train the model and are only visible in your own chat history.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: What generative AI tool does Griffith support?
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Many generative AI tools, especially open or public ones do not guarantee the confidentiality of the information you enter. That means anything you type in could be stored, reused or shared. To protect yourself and others, do not enter: your own personal information; information about others; course materials.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Microsoft Copilot is approved for the following data classifications. You must log in with your Griffith account to ensure data safety. Data safety is not guaranteed without logging in your Griffith account. Unofficial; Official (Public); Official (Internal); Sensitive. Protected data is NOT approved for use in Microsoft Copilot.

artifacts.notes: Approved as narrow student-facing guidance because the evidence directly states self-study use, assessment acknowledgement, and declaration requirements.

Approved because the source uses binding wording that uncited generative AI content always constitutes Academic Misconduct and that representing AI-generated assessment content as one's own work is Academic Misconduct.
Approved as a supported/preferred tool claim tied to Griffith Library wording; it does not assert license terms beyond the source.
Approved as a privacy/data-handling guidance claim because the source explicitly lists information users should not enter into open or public generative AI tools.
Approved as research/eResearch data-classification guidance because the source directly lists Copilot-approved classifications and excludes Protected data.
Approved as an eResearch-scoped AI tool treatment claim; wording is limited to research purposes and public data because the source states the approval does not apply broadly.

artifacts.title: Griffith University AI Policy Staged Findings
artifacts.summary: Staged Griffith University findings are based on official Griffith Library, academic integrity, and eResearch Services pages. The bundle identifies student-facing assessment acknowledgement guidance, academic-misconduct language for uncited generative AI content, Microsoft Copilot support, public/open AI privacy cautions, and eResearch tool/data-classification restrictions.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Direct HTTP access to www.griffith.edu.au returned Cloudflare challenge pages during this run; official page extraction used Firecrawl results only.
Login-required LMS, SharePoint, ServiceNow, and Microsoft Forms resources linked from Griffith pages were not accessed.
The eResearch AI tool claims are scoped to research and data-classification guidance and should not be generalized to learning and teaching uses.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
